name: American Social Hygiene Posters
description: A collection of over 1,200 posters from the American Social Hygiene Association, focusing on public health and social issues. Formats include high-resolution images of the original posters, covering the early to mid-20th century.

name: Harvard Theatre Collection Posters
description: This collection includes over 2,000 posters from the Harvard Theatre Collection, featuring a variety of theatrical performances and events. Formats include digital images of original posters.

name: Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) Posters
institution: The Museum of Modern Art, New York
description: A comprehensive collection of posters from the MoMA archives. The collection features thousands of items, including high-resolution images and detailed metadata.

name: WWI Propaganda Poster Collection
description: This digital collection features over 1,700 posters from World War I, covering a range of topics related to the war effort. Formats include high-resolution images and detailed descriptions.

name: New York Public Library Digital Collections: Posters
description: A diverse collection of posters from the New York Public Library's archives, featuring over 40,000 items related to various themes including social issues, travel, and art.

name: Heinz Architecture Center Digital Collections
description: Features a variety of architectural drawings, photographs, and documents related to the history of architecture. Includes over 10,000 items in various formats.

name: The Getty Research Institute's Digital Collections
description: Offers a vast collection of architectural drawings, photographs, and manuscripts. Includes over 200,000 items ranging from medieval times to the present.

name: The National Archives Architectural Drawings Collection
institution: United States National Archives and Records Administration
description: Provides access to architectural drawings, blueprints, and plans from federal projects. Includes thousands of items in digital formats.

name: The RIBA Collections
description: Showcases a comprehensive collection of architectural drawings, photographs, and models. Includes over 7 million items spanning various eras.

name: The Bauhaus Archive Online Collection
description: Features a vast collection of architectural drawings, photographs, and documents from the Bauhaus School. Includes over 50,000 items in digital formats.

name: Textile and Fashion Collection
description: A digital archive featuring textile swatches, fashion sketches, photographs, and other materials. Includes over 150,000 items in various formats, primarily from the mid-20th century to the present.

name: Victoria & Albert Museum Textile Collection
description: A comprehensive collection of textiles from around the world, including costumes, tapestries, and embroideries. Contains over 80,000 items dating from ancient times to the present day.

name: University of California Los Angeles (UCLA) Digital Collection - Textiles and Costumes
description: This collection features over 4,500 items, including photographs, sketches, and fabric samples from the early 20th century to the present day.

name: Textile Museum Digital Collections
description: Includes a variety of textile objects such as rugs, clothing, and fabric samples. Over 10,000 items from around the world dating back to ancient times.

name: The Miriam and Ira D. Wallach Division of Art, Prints and Photographs: Photography Collection
description: This collection includes over 800,000 photographs from the 19th to the 21st century, featuring works by renowned photographers and covering a wide range of subjects including landscape and urban photography.

name: American Landscape and Architectural Design, 1857-1942
description: A collection featuring over 300 photographs and prints that document the evolution of American landscapes, cityscapes, and architectural designs.

name: Carnegie Mellon University Libraries: Urban Landscape Photographs Collection
description: This collection includes photographs that capture the urban landscapes of Pittsburgh and other cities, offering a visual history from the mid-20th century to the present.

name: Canadian Architectural Photographs
description: This collection consists of architectural photographs primarily from Canada, with a focus on urban landscapes and cityscapes. The images date back to the 19th century.

name: The Photograph Collection
description: This collection includes over 100,000 photographs spanning various subjects and periods. It features urban landscapes and architectural photography from the late 19th century to the present day.

name: The History of Science Collections
description: This collection includes a variety of scientific instruments and diagrams, with over 100 items spanning from the 17th to the 20th century. Formats include manuscripts, drawings, and photographs.

name: The History of Science Collections: Scientific Instruments
institution: Harvard University Library Open Collections Program
description: This collection features historical scientific instruments, including diagrams and descriptions of their use. It contains over 40 items from the late 19th to mid-20th century.

name: Scientific Instruments Collection
institution: The National Museum of American History, Smithsonian Institution
description: This collection includes a wide range of scientific instruments and diagrams from the 18th century to the present. Formats include photographs, drawings, and detailed descriptions.

name: Scientific Instruments Archive
description: This extensive collection features over 30,000 scientific instruments and diagrams. It covers a broad range of eras from the 17th century to the present day.

name: Scientific Instrument Collection
description: This collection includes over 1,500 scientific instruments and diagrams from the 16th to the early 20th century. Formats include drawings, photographs, and detailed descriptions.

name: LuEsther T. Mertz Library Digital Collections
description: Includes over 1 million plant specimens, illustrations, and botanical books. Highlights include historic botanical illustrations from the 18th to 20th centuries.

name: Biodiversity Heritage Library
institution: Global Consortium of Natural History and Botanical Libraries
description: Offers over 180,000 volumes of historical texts in biodiversity. Includes thousands of botanical illustrations and scientific drawings.

name: Botanical Illustrations from the Missouri Botanical Garden
description: Features a collection of botanical illustrations, including over 500,000 images. Covers various plant species and historical botanical art.

name: Herbarium Collection at the Harvard University Herbaria
description: Includes over 5 million specimens and a collection of botanical illustrations. Highlights include historical drawings and scientific documents.

name: University of Michigan Libraries Digital Collections - Botany & Botanical Illustration
description: Features a variety of botanical illustrations and scientific drawings from the 18th to 20th centuries. Includes over 10,000 items in various formats.

name: The Internet Archive Audio Collection
description: A vast collection of free audio books, poetry, music, and spoken word recordings from around the world. Contains over 200,000 items in various formats including MP3, WAV, and FLAC.

name: University of California, Santa Barbara Cylinder Audio Archive
description: A collection of historical recorded sound from the late 19th to the early 20th century, featuring over 8,400 cylinders and 600 recordings.

name: British Library Sounds
description: A diverse collection of sounds and music recordings from around the world. Includes over 50,000 items such as interviews, environmental sounds, and musical performances.

name: National Jukebox (Library of Congress)
description: Historical recordings from the Victor Talking Machine Company, featuring over 10,000 recordings of 78-rpm discs. Includes classical music, jazz, popular songs, and spoken word.

name: Phillip A. Schuyler Digital Archive at the University of Wisconsin-Madison Libraries
description: A collection featuring over 2,000 audio recordings from the personal archives of Phillip A. Schuyler, focusing on experimental music, interviews with composers and musicians.

name: History of Science Collections
description: This collection includes scientific instruments, diagrams, and related materials dating back to the 17th century. It features a variety of formats including images, texts, and artifacts.

name: Science & Technology Collections
description: The Cambridge University Digital Library includes scientific instruments and diagrams, with a focus on historical and modern science. The collection features over 20,000 images in various formats.

name: Scientific Instruments Collection
institution: National Museum of American History (Smithsonian Institution)
description: This collection includes a wide array of scientific instruments and diagrams, providing an in-depth look at the evolution of science and technology. The collection features over 100,000 items.

name: The Science Museum Group Collection Online
description: This archive includes over 325,000 items related to science and technology, including scientific instruments, diagrams, and photographs. The collection spans multiple eras.

name: The Scientific Instrument Collection of the University of Toronto Libraries
description: This collection includes a variety of scientific instruments and diagrams, with a focus on historical and modern science. The collection features over 100 images in various formats.

name: Anatomical Atlas Collection
institution: University of Toronto Thomas Fisher Rare Book Library
description: A collection of over 60 anatomical atlases and illustrations, covering a wide range of eras from the early 17th century to the late 20th century. Includes detailed illustrations in various formats.

name: Medical Heritage Library
description: A digital curation project from the National Library of Medicine, featuring over 60,000 digitized books and journals. Includes high-quality anatomical illustrations from the 19th century onwards.

name: Wellcome Collection Medical Images
description: A rich collection of over 65,000 high-quality digital images from the history of medicine. Includes illustrations from medical texts and historical manuscripts.

name: Anatomical Illustration Collection
description: The NLM's Image Archive includes over 40,000 illustrations from medical texts and anatomical atlases.

name: Gray’s Anatomy Illustrations Collection
description: A digital collection from the University of Toronto that includes over 100 illustrations from Gray's Anatomy, a seminal work in anatomical studies.

name: Fashion Plates and Costume Designs
description: This collection includes approximately 2,500 fashion plates from the late 18th to early 20th century, featuring high-resolution scans and detailed metadata. The plates showcase various fashion trends over a span of centuries.

name: Costume Institute Fashion Plate Collection
description: This collection contains over 16,000 fashion plates from the late 18th to early 20th century. The digital archive includes high-quality images and comprehensive descriptions of each plate.

name: Fashion Plates from the New York Public Library
institution: New York Public Library Digital Collections
description: The collection includes over 1,500 fashion plates from the late 19th to early 20th century, showcasing various designs and styles of clothing.

name: Fashion Institute of Design & Merchandising Digital Archive
description: This collection features a wide range of fashion plates, costume designs, and historical garments from various eras. It includes both high-resolution images and detailed descriptions.

name: Bryant College Costume Designs
institution: University of Southern California Digital Library
description: The collection includes approximately 5,000 fashion plates and costume designs from the late 19th to early 20th century. The digital archive provides high-resolution images and detailed metadata for each item.

name: Building America: Architecture and Design Collection
description: This collection features photographs, blueprints, and documents related to American architecture from the 19th and 20th centuries, focusing on Texas buildings, with around 450 items.

name: Getty Research Institute Architecture Archives
description: This extensive archive includes drawings, photographs, and documents related to modern architecture, with over 1 million items dating from the mid-20th century.

name: The Image of the City: Photographs by Berenice Abbott
description: This collection contains over 700 photographs taken by Berenice Abbott of New York City, documenting the city's architecture and urban life from the 1930s.

name: The Avery Architectural & Fine Arts Library Digital Collections
description: This collection includes architectural drawings, photographs, and documents spanning various eras and styles, with over 50,000 items.

name: Architectural Designs of the Historic American Buildings Survey (HABS)
description: The HABS collection features measured drawings, large-format photographs, and written histories of historic structures across the United States from 1933 to the present.

name: Digital Public Library of America
description: The DPLA provides access to over 30 million digital objects from libraries, archives, and museums across the United States. The collection includes a wide range of formats such as photographs, books, maps, news footage, oral histories, personal letters, museum artifacts, artwork, government documents, and more.

name: MIT Libraries Visual Collections
description: The MIT Libraries Visual Collections include over 50,000 images from the university's archives and special collections. The collection features photographs, illustrations, architectural drawings, and more.

name: The Digital Scriptorium
description: A growing image database of medieval and renaissance manuscripts that enables users to search across the collections of participating institutions. The collection includes high-resolution images of illuminated manuscripts.

name: Metropolitan Museum of Art Open Access Collection
description: Over 406,523 images and data about public domain artworks in the Met's collection.

name: Harvard Art Museums Digital Collections
description: Over 250,000 high-resolution images of artworks from the collections of the Fogg Museum, Busch-Reisinger Museum, and Arthur M. Sackler Museum.

name: National Gallery of Art - NGA Images
description: Over 50,000 digital images of public domain artworks from the National Gallery of Art collection.

name: Astronomical Plate Collection
description: This collection includes over 30,000 photographic plates from the University of Michigan's Department of Astronomy, dating from the late 19th to the mid-20th century.

name: Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics Plate Stack Collection
description: This collection includes over half a million glass plate images, featuring astronomical observations from the 19th and early 20th centuries.

name: Astronomy Photographs of Egypt
description: This collection features astronomy photographs and artifacts from ancient Egyptian astronomical observations.

name: Astronomical Photographic Data Archive (APDA)
description: The APDA includes over 100,000 photographic plates from the Royal Observatory, capturing celestial observations from the late 20th century.

name: Media Burn Archive
description: A collection of activist videos from the 1960s to the present, featuring over 6000 titles in various formats including VHS and DVD.

name: British Film Institute National Archive
description: The largest film archive in the world, with over 250,000 films including silent films, documentaries, and experimental cinema.

name: CineFiles: A Database of Film Documents
description: A collection of over 1 million film-related documents, including reviews, interviews, and articles spanning the history of cinema.

name: The Internet Archive Moving Image Archive
description: A vast collection of free movies, films, and videos including feature films, shorts, documentaries, newsreels, and more.

name: The Prelinger Archives: The Ephemeral Film Collection
description: A collection of over 60,000 ephemeral films including advertising, educational, industrial, and amateur works.

name: Folk Art Digital Collection
description: A collection of over 10,000 images from the University of Michigan’s Folk Art collection, including photographs and drawings from various folk art traditions around the world, spanning a wide range of eras.

name: Folk Art Collection
description: The National Museum of American History's Folk Art collection includes over 10,000 objects, ranging from quilts and textiles to wooden carvings and paintings. The digital archive provides access to images and descriptions.

name: The Vernacular Architecture Collection
description: This collection features photographs, drawings, and documents related to vernacular architecture. It includes over 12,000 items spanning from the early 20th century to the present.

name: Folk Art of Latin America and the Caribbean
description: This collection includes thousands of images, manuscripts, and audio recordings documenting folk art traditions in Latin America and the Caribbean. The materials span various formats and eras.

name: Digital Folk Art Collection
institution: Smithsonian Institution Archives of American Art
description: The Archives of American Art at the Smithsonian Institution contains a vast collection of folk art, including photographs, documents, and artwork. It includes over 80,000 items from the mid-19th century to the present.

name: Carnegie Mellon University Medical Illustration Collection
description: This collection features over 1,500 medical and anatomical illustrations from the 16th to the 20th century. Formats include engravings, lithographs, and photographs.

name: National Library of Medicine Digital Collections
description: Offers a vast array of digitized medical illustrations, manuscripts, and historic images. Includes over 800,000 items in various formats from the Middle Ages to the present.

name: University of Toronto Medical Artifacts Collection
description: This collection includes medical and anatomical illustrations, surgical instruments, and other artifacts dating back to the 19th century.

name: Wellcome Collection Digital Archive
description: A diverse collection of over 30,000 items including medical illustrations, manuscripts, and photographs. It spans various eras from the Middle Ages to modern times.

name: The College of Physicians of Philadelphia Digital Library
institution: The College of Physicians of Philadelphia
description: This digital archive contains a rich collection of historical medical texts, illustrations, and manuscripts. It features over 10,000 items from the 16th century to the present.

name: USGS Historical Topographic Maps
description: The USGS Historical Topographic Map Collection includes over 200,000 historical maps dating back to the late 19th century. The collection is fully browsable and downloadable in various formats including GeoTIFF and PDF.

name: Geological Survey of Canada's Library Digitization Projects
description: The collection includes over 10,000 geoscientific maps and reports from the late 19th century to the present. It provides access to digitized versions of historical geological surveys.

name: British Geological Survey's GeoScenic Collection
description: This collection includes over 130,000 aerial photographs of the UK, dating from the 1940s to the present. It provides a comprehensive visual record of geological changes and land use.

name: Geological Survey of Norway's Digital Library
description: The collection includes over 30,000 geological maps, reports, and documents dating back to the late 19th century. It offers a variety of formats including PDFs and GeoTIFFs.

name: University of Texas Libraries' Historical Maps Collection
institution: University of Texas at Austin Libraries
description: This collection includes over 2,500 maps and atlases from the 16th century to the present. It features a variety of geological surveys and maps.
